text: Tell me about a time you had to lead a project without formal authority over the people involved.
Describe a situation where two stakeholders wanted conflicting things from your product. How did you resolve it?
Walk me through how you would prioritize a backlog when everything is labeled 'urgent' by different teams.
Tell me about a release that went wrong. What happened and what did you change afterward?
How do you decide what NOT to build, given limited engineering capacity?
Describe how you'd design a system to detect and alert on late shipments across a global supply chain.
Tell me about a time a project's scope changed midway through. How did you handle communicating that to stakeholders?
Design a rate limiter for a public API. Walk me through your approach.
Tell me about the most complex bug you've debugged. How did you track it down?
How would you design a URL shortening service that needs to handle millions of requests per day?
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate's technical approach. What did you do?
Tell me about a time you had to learn a new technology quickly to ship something on time.
Explain how you would design the memory architecture for a multi-agent LLM system handling long conversations.
How would you evaluate whether a RAG pipeline's retrieval step is actually helping versus hurting answer quality?
Tell me about a time an AI system you built produced an unexpected or wrong output in production. What did you do?
How would you decide when to use a multi-agent architecture versus a single well-prompted agent?
Describe how you would reduce hallucinations in an agent that has access to external tools.
Tell me about a time you received tough feedback. How did you respond?
Describe a time you had to make a decision with incomplete information.
Tell me about a conflict you had with a coworker and how you resolved it.
